channel.title: Frontion Geopolitical Headlines
channel.description: Real-time geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸🇩🇪 Trump Withdraws 5,000 Troops from Germany as NATO Tensions Deepen
item.description: The Pentagon announced a withdrawal of 5,000 U.S. troops from Germany, a move European officials warn undermines NATO cohesion amid the Iran crisis. The decision follows German criticism of U.S. policy and highlights the limits of European efforts to keep Washington committed to the alliance. Source: Reuters. Category: Europe.

item.title: 💰🇺🇸 Fed Policy Constrained by Iran War Uncertainty, Says Kashkari
item.description: Minneapolis Fed President Neel Kashkari warned that the ongoing Iran conflict limits the Federal Reserve's ability to provide guidance on interest rate policy, citing risks of higher inflation and economic damage the longer the war persists. Source: Reuters. Category: Economy.

item.title: 🚗🇩🇪🇺🇸 German Carmakers Slump as Trump Hikes EU Auto Tariffs to 25%
item.description: Shares in German automakers fell sharply after President Trump raised U.S. tariffs on imported European cars from 15% to 25%, dealing a fresh blow to the sector already struggling with global supply chain disruptions. Source: Reuters. Category: Economy.

item.title: 🇪🇺⚔️ US Military Presence in Europe Under Spotlight After Germany Drawdown
item.description: An AP News analysis examines the scope and strategic importance of U.S. military deployments across Europe as Trump's troop withdrawal from Germany raises questions about America's commitment to NATO deterrence against Russia. Source: AP News. Category: Defense Industry.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden Arrests Chinese Captain of Russia-Linked Tanker
item.description: The Chinese captain of a seized oil tanker with suspected Russian ties was placed under arrest in Sweden on suspicion of carrying false documents and violating seaworthiness laws, the latest action against Russia's shadow fleet in the Baltic Sea. Source: Reuters. Category: Europe.

item.title: 🇪🇺 ECB's Kazimir: June Rate Hike All But Inevitable
item.description: European Central Bank policymaker Peter Kazimir said a June rate hike is virtually certain as higher energy costs from the Iran war spread to the broader economy, with no positive developments on the horizon. Source: Reuters. Category: Economy.

item.title: 🛢️ Most Hormuz Shipping at Standstill Despite US Pledge
item.description: No increased vessel traffic through the Strait of Hormuz was visible Monday, a day after President Trump announced efforts to free stranded ships, as Iran's military warned against uncoordinated movement. Source: Reuters. Category: Energy.

item.title: 📈 Gulf Markets Rise on Hopes for Middle East Resolution
item.description: Most Gulf stock exchanges closed slightly higher Monday, supported by limited signs that the Middle East conflict may be resolved, even as global markets remain jittery. Source: Reuters. Category: Economy.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Launches Effort to Reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: US forces began guiding commercial ships through the Strait of Hormuz under "Project Freedom," with two US-flagged vessels successfully transiting, though Iran warned the effort would violate the ceasefire. Source: AP News. Category: US-Iran War.

item.title: 🇮🇷 IRGC publishes new map of Strait of Hormuz control zone as shipping remains at standstill
item.description: Iran Revolutionary Guard Corps released a revised map delineating its claimed control area in the Strait of Hormuz, even as virtually no commercial tankers transited the waterway despite Trump's pledge to reopen it. Only one sanctioned LPG carrier and a few cargo ships moved through on Monday. Source: Jerusalem Post. Category: US-Iran War.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon parliament speaker Berri rejects negotiations with Israel until war halts
item.description: Nabih Berri, Lebanon's most senior Shi'ite politician and Hezbollah ally, said no talks with Israel are possible while fighting continues in the south. Israel ordered evacuations of four more villages as the ceasefire fails to hold. Source: Al-Monitor. Category: Middle East.

item.title: 🇦🇹 Austria expels three Russian embassy staff over suspected signals intelligence operations
item.description: Austria expelled three Russian diplomats linked to satellite dishes on diplomatic buildings in Vienna used to intercept data from international organizations like the OSCE and UN. The officials have already left the country. Source: Kyiv Independent. Category: Europe.

item.title: 🇫🇮 Zelensky asks Finland to sign Drone Deal after Ukrainian UAVs violate Finnish airspace
item.description: Finnish PM Orpo raised the issue of Ukrainian drones entering Finnish airspace during bilateral talks in Yerevan. Zelensky proposed a Drone Deal agreement and offered to share Ukrainian drone technology expertise with Finland. Source: YLE. Category: Ukraine-Russia War.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Türkiye's National Cyber Security Board holds inaugural meeting chaired by Erdoğan
item.description: Turkey's newly established Board of Cyber Security convened for the first time, with President Erdoğan expected to chair. The board includes intelligence, defense, and foreign affairs officials, and will set policies for critical infrastructure protection and cybersecurity human resources. Source: Daily Sabah. Category: Cyber.

item.title: 🇬🇾🇻🇪🏛️ Guyana and Venezuela Return to ICJ Over Essequibo Border Dispute
item.description: Guyana told the International Court of Justice that 70% of its territory is at stake in a historic border dispute with Venezuela over the resource-rich Essequibo region. The week of hearings continues years of legal battles. Source: AP News. Category: Latin America.

item.title: 🚢⚠️🌍 UN Warns 20,000 Seafarers at Risk as Strait of Hormuz Standoff Continues
item.description: The UN International Maritime Organization reported at least 41 incidents in the Gulf region since late February. Around 800 ships with 20,000 crew members remain vulnerable amid the US-Iran conflict. Source: UN News. Category: US-Iran War.

item.title: 🇪🇺🛡️ NATO chief Rutte says Europe ready to help clear mines from Strait of Hormuz
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said European governments "got the message" from Trump on the Iran conflict and are prepared to deploy mine hunters and minesweepers in the "next phase" of the operation to restore freedom of navigation. Source: Irish Times. Category: Diplomacy.

item.title: 🇮🇳🏛️ Modi's BJP wins historic victory in West Bengal state elections
item.description: India's ruling BJP has conquered one of its toughest political frontiers, winning West Bengal after 15 years of TMC rule. The victory marks a major expansion of Modi's political influence into eastern India, with the state's electorate larger than Germany's. Source: BBC. Category: Asia-Pacific.

item.title: 🚢🦠 Three dead from suspected hantavirus outbreak on Atlantic cruise ship
item.description: Three people have died and at least three others are ill after a suspected hantavirus outbreak on a Dutch cruise ship off Cape Verde. The WHO confirmed one case and is coordinating medical evacuations while saying the risk to the public is low. Source: AP News. Category: Africa.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Luxury Moscow Tower Hit by Ukrainian Drone Strike Ahead of Victory Day Parade
item.description: A Ukrainian drone strike hit the Mosfilm Tower in an elite Moscow neighborhood approximately 7km from Red Square and 3km from the Russian Defense Ministry, marking one of the deepest strikes into central Moscow's residential core. Source: Kyiv Independent. Category: Ukraine-Russia War.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany Prepares Wartime Supply Lines as Defense Minister Cites Paradigm Shift
item.description: Berlin is ramping up military logistics infrastructure in what Defense Minister Pistorius calls a "paradigm shift," but structural and bureaucratic hurdles remain significant for the Bundeswehr's transformation. Source: Kyiv Post. Category: Europe.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Withdrawing 5,000 Troops from Germany as Iran War Rift With Europe Widens
item.description: The Trump administration is pulling 5,000 troops from Germany as a rift over the Iran war widens between President Trump and European allies. Key Republican chairmen warn the move sends the "wrong signal" to Russia. Source: Defense News. Category: US Politics.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel to Buy Extra F-15IA and F-35I Squadrons After High-Tempo Middle East Operations
item.description: After years of high-tempo operations in the Middle East, Israel has locked in plans to further expand its airpower with new F-15IA and F-35I fighter squadrons. Source: The War Zone. Category: Defense Industry.

item.title: 🍽️⛽ US Restaurant Sales Drop as Iran War Pushes Gasoline Prices Higher
item.description: Major US restaurant chains including Wingstop and Domino's reported weaker-than-expected sales growth as soaring gasoline prices from the US-Iran conflict forced customers to cut back on discretionary spending. Source: Reuters. Category: Economy.

item.title: 🛢️💥 Oil Prices Jump 5% After Report of US Warship Hit by Missiles
item.description: Oil prices surged approximately 5% after Iran's Fars news agency reported an incident with a US warship in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over prolonged disruption to the vital oil transit route. Source: Reuters. Category: Energy.
